title: silene ammophila subsp. carpathae
intro: Silene ammophila subsp. carpathae, also known as Carpathian catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its pink or white flowers and long stamens. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Silene ammophila subsp. carpathae include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or dried herb, and can be consumed as a tea or added to topical creams and ointments for its therapeutic benefits.
